RequestBody.input: What is my username in Github profile?

tools.name: add_comment_to_pending_review
tools.description: Add review comment to the requester's latest pending pull request review. A pending review needs to already exist to call this (check with the user if not sure).

body.description: The text of the review comment

line.description: The line of the blob in the pull request diff that the comment applies to. For multi-line comments, the last line of the range

path.description: The relative path to the file that necessitates a comment

side.description: The side of the diff to comment on. LEFT indicates the previous state, RIGHT indicates the new state

startLine.description: For multi-line comments, the first line of the range that the comment applies to

startSide.description: For multi-line comments, the starting side of the diff that the comment applies to. LEFT indicates the previous state, RIGHT indicates the new state

subjectType.description: The level at which the comment is targeted

tools.name: add_issue_comment
tools.description: Add a comment to a specific issue in a GitHub repository. Use this tool to add comments to pull requests as well (in this case pass pull request number as issue_number), but only if user is not asking specifically to add review comments.

tools.name: add_reply_to_pull_request_comment
tools.description: Add a reply to an existing pull request comment. This creates a new comment that is linked as a reply to the specified comment.

commentId.description: The ID of the comment to reply to

tools.name: create_branch
tools.description: Create a new branch in a GitHub repository

tools.name: create_or_update_file
tools.description:
Create or update a single file in a GitHub repository. 
If updating, you should provide the SHA of the file you want to update. Use this tool to create or update a file in a GitHub repository remotely; do not use it for local file operations.

In order to obtain the SHA of original file version before updating, use the following git command:
git rev-parse <branch>:<path to file>

SHA MUST be provided for existing file updates.

branch.description: Branch to create/update the file in

path.description: Path where to create/update the file

sha.description: The blob SHA of the file being replaced. Required if the file already exists.

tools.name: create_pull_request
tools.description: Create a new pull request in a GitHub repository.

reviewers.description: GitHub usernames or ORG/team-slug team reviewers to request reviews from

tools.name: create_repository
tools.description: Create a new GitHub repository in your account or specified organization

organization.description: Organization to create the repository in (omit to create in your personal account)

private.description: Whether the repository should be private. Defaults to true (private) when omitted.

tools.name: delete_file
tools.description: Delete a file from a GitHub repository

branch.description: Branch to delete the file from

path.description: Path to the file to delete

tools.name: fork_repository
tools.description: Fork a GitHub repository to your account or specified organization

tools.name: get_commit
tools.description: Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository

detail.description: Level of detail to include for changed files. "none" omits stats and files entirely. "stats" (default) includes per-file metadata: filename, status, and lines-of-code counts (additions, deletions, changes), with no patch content. "full_patch" additionally includes the unified diff content for each file and can be very large.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch name, or tag name

tools.name: get_file_contents
tools.description: Get the contents of a file or directory from a GitHub repository

ref.description: Accepts optional git refs such as `refs/tags/{tag}`, `refs/heads/{branch}` or `refs/pull/{pr_number}/head`

sha.description: Accepts optional commit SHA. If specified, it will be used instead of ref

tools.name: get_label
tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.name: get_latest_release
tools.description: Get the latest release in a GitHub repository

tools.name: get_me
tools.description: Get details of the authenticated GitHub user. Use this when a request is about the user's own profile for GitHub. Or when information is missing to build other tool calls.

tools.name: get_release_by_tag
tools.description: Get a specific release by its tag name in a GitHub repository

tools.name: get_tag
tools.description: Get details about a specific git tag in a GitHub repository

tools.name: get_team_members
tools.description: Get member usernames of a specific team in an organization. Limited to organizations accessible with current credentials

org.description: Organization login (owner) that contains the team.

tools.name: get_teams
tools.description: Get details of the teams the user is a member of. Limited to organizations accessible with current credentials

user.description: Username to get teams for. If not provided, uses the authenticated user.

tools.name: issue_read
tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_write
tools.description: Create a new or update an existing issue in a GitHub repository.

assignees.description: Usernames to assign to this issue

duplicate_of.description: Issue number that this issue is a duplicate of. Only used when state_reason is 'duplicate'.

issue_fields.description: Issue field values to set or clear. Each item requires 'field_name' and exactly one of 'value', 'field_option_name', or 'delete: true'.

delete.description: Set to true to clear this field's current value on the issue. Cannot be combined with 'value' or 'field_option_name'.

field_name.description: Issue field name (case-insensitive). Must match a field returned by list_issue_fields for this repository or its organization.

field_option_name.description: Option name for single-select fields. Validated against the field's options before the API call. Cannot be combined with 'value' or 'delete'.

value.description: Value to set. Use for text, number, and date fields (date as YYYY-MM-DD). For single-select fields, prefer 'field_option_name' so the option is validated before the API call. Cannot be combined with 'field_option_name' or 'delete'.

labels.description: Labels to apply to this issue

method.description:
Write operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
- 'create' - creates a new issue.
- 'update' - updates an existing issue.

state_reason.description: Reason for the state change. Ignored unless state is changed.

type.description: Type of this issue. Only use if issue types are enabled for this repository. Use list_issue_types tool to get valid type values for this repository or its owner organization. If the repository doesn't support issue types, omit this parameter.

tools.name: list_branches
tools.description: List branches in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_commits
tools.description: Get list of commits of a branch in a GitHub repository. Returns at least 30 results per page by default, but can return more if specified using the perPage parameter (up to 100).

author.description: Author username or email address to filter commits by

path.description: Only commits containing this file path will be returned

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

sha.description: Commit SHA, branch or tag name to list commits of. If not provided, uses the default branch of the repository. If a commit SHA is provided, will list commits up to that SHA.

since.description: Only commits after this date will be returned (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ or YYYY-MM-DD)

until.description: Only commits before this date will be returned (ISO 8601 format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ or YYYY-MM-DD)

tools.name: list_issue_fields
tools.description: List issue fields for a repository or organization. Returns field definitions including name, type (text, number, date, single_select), and for single_select fields the list of valid option names. When repo is omitted, returns org-level fields directly.

owner.description: The account owner of the repository or organization. The name is not case sensitive.

repo.description: The name of the repository. When provided, returns fields for this specific repository (inherited from its organization). When omitted, returns org-level fields directly.

tools.name: list_issue_types
tools.description: List supported issue types for a repository or its owner organization. When repo is omitted, returns org-level issue types directly.

owner.description: The account owner of the repository or organization.

repo.description: The name of the repository. When provided, returns issue types for this specific repository. When omitted, returns org-level issue types directly.

tools.name: list_issues
tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the cursor from the previous response.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

field_filters.description: Filter by custom issue field values. Each entry takes a field_name and a value; the server looks up the field and coerces the value to its type (single-select option name, text, number, or YYYY-MM-DD date).

field_name.description: Name of the custom field (e.g. "Priority"). Case-insensitive.

value.description: Value to filter on. For single-select fields, the option name (e.g. "P1"). For dates, YYYY-MM-DD. For numbers, the numeric value as a string. For text, the text value.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_pull_requests
tools.description: List pull requests in a GitHub repository. If the user specifies an author, then DO NOT use this tool and use the search_pull_requests tool instead.

head.description: Filter by head user/org and branch

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_releases
tools.description: List releases in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_repository_collaborators
tools.description: List collaborators of a GitHub repository. Results are paginated; the response includes `nextPage`, `prevPage`, `firstPage`, and `lastPage` fields. To get the next page, use the `nextPage` value as the `page` parameter.

affiliation.description: Filter by affiliation. Can be one of: 'outside' (outside collaborators), 'direct' (all with permissions regardless of org membership), 'all' (all collaborators). Default: 'all'

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (default 30, min 1, max 100)

tools.name: list_tags
tools.description: List git tags in a GitHub repository

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: merge_pull_request
tools.description: Merge a pull request in a GitHub repository.

tools.name: pull_request_read
tools.description: Get information on a specific pull request in GitHub repository.

after.description: Cursor for pagination, used only by the get_review_comments method. Pass the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo to fetch the next page.

method.description:
Action to specify what pull request data needs to be retrieved from GitHub. 
Possible options: 
 1. get - Get details of a specific pull request.
 2. get_diff - Get the diff of a pull request.
 3. get_status - Get combined commit status of a head commit in a pull request.
 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 5. get_commits - Get the list of commits on a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 6. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.
 7. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 8. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 9. get_check_runs - Get check runs for the head commit of a pull request. Check runs are the individual CI/CD jobs and checks that run on the PR.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: pull_request_review_write
tools.description:
Create and/or submit, delete review of a pull request.

Available methods:
- create: Create a new review of a pull request. If "event" parameter is provided, the review is submitted. If "event" is omitted, a pending review is created.
- submit_pending: Submit an existing pending review of a pull request. This requires that a pending review exists for the current user on the specified pull request. The "body" and "event" parameters are used when submitting the review.
- delete_pending: Delete an existing pending review of a pull request. This requires that a pending review exists for the current user on the specified pull request.
- resolve_thread: Resolve a review thread. Requires only "threadId" parameter with the thread's node ID (e.g., PRRT_kwDOxxx). The owner, repo, and pullNumber parameters are not used for this method. Resolving an already-resolved thread is a no-op.
- unresolve_thread: Unresolve a previously resolved review thread. Requires only "threadId" parameter. The owner, repo, and pullNumber parameters are not used for this method. Unresolving an already-unresolved thread is a no-op.

method.description: The write operation to perform on pull request review.

threadId.description: The node ID of the review thread (e.g., PRRT_kwDOxxx). Required for resolve_thread and unresolve_thread methods. Get thread IDs from pull_request_read with method get_review_comments.

tools.name: push_files
tools.description: Push multiple files to a GitHub repository in a single commit

files.description: Array of file objects to push, each object with path (string) and content (string)

tools.name: request_copilot_review
tools.description: Request a GitHub Copilot code review for a pull request. Use this for automated feedback on pull requests, usually before requesting a human reviewer.

tools.name: run_secret_scanning
tools.description:
Scan files, content, or recent changes for secrets such as API keys, passwords, tokens, and credentials.

This tool is intended for targeted scans of specific files, snippets, or diffs provided directly as content. The files parameter accepts either a single string or an array of strings containing raw file contents or diff hunks, and returns detected secrets with their locations and related secret scanning metadata. Content must not be empty. For full repository scanning, other mechanisms are available.

Caveats:

- Only files within the codebase should be scanned. Files outside of the codebase should not be sent.
- Files listed in .gitignore should be skipped.

files.description: A single string or an array of strings containing file contents, snippets, or diff hunks to scan for secrets. These must be raw contents, not repository file paths.

tools.name: search_code
tools.description: Fast and precise code search across ALL GitHub repositories using GitHub's native search engine. Best for finding exact symbols, functions, classes, or specific code patterns.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query (GitHub code search REST). Implicit AND between terms; supports `OR`, `NOT`, and `"quoted phrase"` for exact match. Qualifiers: `repo:owner/repo`, `org:`, `user:`, `language:`, `path:dir` (prefix match), `filename:exact.ext`, `extension:`, `in:file`, `in:path`, `size:`, `is:archived`, `is:fork`. Max 256 chars. Examples: `WithContext language:go org:github`; `"package main" repo:o/r`; `func extension:go path:cmd repo:o/r`; `NOT TODO language:go repo:o/r`.

tools.name: search_commits
tools.description: Search for commits across GitHub repositories using GitHub's commit search syntax. Useful for finding specific changes, authors, or messages across one or many repositories. Searches the default branch only.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Commit search query (GitHub commit search REST). Searches commit messages on the default branch only. Scope the search with `repo:owner/repo`, `org:`, or `user:` (queries without a scope qualifier match across all of GitHub and are usually not what you want). Other qualifiers: `author:`, `committer:`, `author-name:`, `committer-name:`, `author-email:`, `committer-email:`, `author-date:`, `committer-date:` (supports `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`, and `YYYY-MM-DD..YYYY-MM-DD` ranges), `merge:true|false`, `hash:`, `tree:`, `parent:`, `is:public`. Examples: `repo:owner/repo fix panic`; `org:github author:defunkt committer-date:>=2024-01-01`; `"refactor cache" repo:o/r`; `hash:abc1234 repo:o/r`.

sort.description: Sort by author or committer date (defaults to best match)

tools.name: search_issues
tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_pull_requests
tools.description: Search for pull requests in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:pr

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only pull requests for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub pull request search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only pull requests for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_repositories
tools.description: Find GitHub repositories by name, description, readme, topics, or other metadata. Perfect for discovering projects, finding examples, or locating specific repositories across GitHub.

minimal_output.description: Return minimal repository information (default: true). When false, returns full GitHub API repository objects.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Repository search query. Examples: 'machine learning in:name stars:>1000 language:python', 'topic:react', 'user:facebook'. Supports advanced search syntax for precise filtering.

sort.description: Sort repositories by field, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_users
tools.description: Find GitHub users by username, real name, or other profile information. Useful for locating developers, contributors, or team members.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: User search query. Examples: 'john smith', 'location:seattle', 'followers:>100'. Search is automatically scoped to type:user.

sort.description: Sort users by number of followers or repositories, or when the person joined GitHub.

tools.name: sub_issue_write
tools.description: Add a sub-issue to a parent issue in a GitHub repository.

after_id.description: The ID of the sub-issue to be prioritized after (either after_id OR before_id should be specified)

before_id.description: The ID of the sub-issue to be prioritized before (either after_id OR before_id should be specified)

issue_number.description: The number of the parent issue

method.description:
The action to perform on a single sub-issue
Options are:
- 'add' - add a sub-issue to a parent issue in a GitHub repository.
- 'remove' - remove a sub-issue from a parent issue in a GitHub repository.
- 'reprioritize' - change the order of sub-issues within a parent issue in a GitHub repository. Use either 'after_id' or 'before_id' to specify the new position.

replace_parent.description: When true, replaces the sub-issue's current parent issue. Use with 'add' method only.

sub_issue_id.description: The ID of the sub-issue to add. ID is not the same as issue number

tools.name: update_pull_request
tools.description: Update an existing pull request in a GitHub repository.

draft.description: Mark pull request as draft (true) or ready for review (false)

reviewers.description: GitHub usernames or ORG/team-slug team reviewers to request reviews from

tools.name: update_pull_request_branch
tools.description: Update the branch of a pull request with the latest changes from the base branch.

expectedHeadSha.description: The expected SHA of the pull request's HEAD ref
